lines.en_lit: does not bring a heifer whose neck is broken. And furthermore, it is written “it be not known who had smitten him,” and it was known who had smitten him. Rather, order to increase weeping.

The father of the boy came and found that he was convulsing. He said: May be an atonement for you. my son is still alive, to teach you that the ritual purity of utensils of more concern to them than the shedding of blood. A dilemma was raised before bloodshed had become trivialized but purity of utensils remained where it was or perhaps bloodshed remained where it was but purity of vessels strict,

Come hear adduces a biblical teaching “Furthermore, Manasseh spilled innocent blood,” conclude from this it was bloodshed that had become trivialized, and purity of utensils remained where it had been.
The Sages taught “And he shall put off and put on other garments, and carry the ashes out I understand similar to Yom Kippur, that he remove his sacred garments and put on non-sacred garments.

the verse states: “And he shall put off his garments, and put on other garments,” juxtaposing the garments he puts on to the garments he takes off. just as there, sacred garments, so too here, sacred garments.
If so, what the verse states: Other of lower quality than Rabbi Eliezer says other and carry out teaches that priests with blemishes, are eligible to carry out the ashes.

The Master said Other of lower quality than in accordance with was taught the school of Rabbi Yishmael, as it was taught the school of Rabbi Yishmael: Clothes as he was cooking food for his master should not he pours a cup for his master,

Reish Lakish said: Just as a dispute with regard to carrying out so too, there is a dispute with regard to the removal of the ashes But Rabbi Yoḥanan said: dispute with regard to carrying out but all agree that the removal is a service
What is the reason behind of Reish Lakish? said to you: If it enters your mind is a service, Do you have service that may be performed with two garments
And Rabbi Yoḥanan’s The Merciful One reveals the tunic and the trousers the same is true the mitre and the belt.

what is different about these “his linen garment,” “his garment” [middo], to his size [middato] “linen trousers,” that which was taught From where that no should precede the trousers? As it is stated: “And his linen trousers shall he put on his flesh,”
And Reish Lakish, his garment, according to his size the Merciful One uses the expression “his garment,” that no should precede the trousers derived “on his flesh.”
Let us say parallel to tanna’im. on his flesh.” What the verse states: “Shall he put on”? to include mitre and belt, the removal Rabbi Yehuda.
Rabbi Dosa says: to include the High Priest’s clothes on Yom Kippur, they are acceptable common priests

Rabbi says: There are two refutations One the belt of High Priest is not the belt of common priest, And furthermore, garments that you used the most severe sanctity, you will use them services of lesser sanctity Instead What, the verse states “shall he put on”?
